template.template: Additional Template App Microsoft Exchange Server 2010
template.name: Additional Template App Microsoft Exchange Server 2010
template.description:
MS Exchange Server template
LLD: Databases (with PowerShell script)

item.name: Exchange mounted databases count
item.description:
Shows the number of active database copies on the server. MSExchange Active Manager(_total)\Database Mounted
"\MSExchange Active Manager(_total)\Database Mounted" "\11196(_total)\11198"

trigger.name: Number of mounted databases has changed

item.name: ActiveSync ping command pending
item.description:
Shows the number of ping commands currently pending in the queue. MSExchange ActiveSync\Ping Commands Pending
"\MSExchange ActiveSync\Ping Commands Pending" "\10946\11090"

item.name: ActiveSync requests per second
item.description:
Shows the number of HTTP requests received from the client via ASP.NET per second. Determines the current Exchange ActiveSync request rate. Used only to determine current user load. MSExchange ActiveSync\Requests/sec
"\MSExchange ActiveSync\Requests/sec" "\10946\10964"

item.name: ActiveSync sync commands per second
item.description:
Shows the number of sync commands processed per second. Clients use this command to synchronize items within a folder. MSExchange ActiveSync\Sync Commands/sec
"\MSExchange ActiveSync\Sync Commands/sec" "\10946\10980"

item.name: Autodiscover requests per second
item.description:
Shows the number of Autodiscover service requests processed each second. Determines current user load. MSExchangeAutodiscover\Requests/sec
"\MSExchangeAutodiscover\Requests/sec" "\8034\8038"

item.name: Availability Service availability requests per second
item.description:
Shows the number of requests serviced per second. The request can be only for free/ busy information or include suggestions. One request may contain multiple mailboxes. Determines the rate at which Availability service requests are occurring. MSExchange Availability Service\Availability Requests (sec)
"\MSExchange Availability Service\Availability Requests (sec)" "\10446\10468"

item.name: OWA current unique users
item.description:
Shows the number of unique users currently logged on to Outlook Web App. This value monitors the number of unique active user sessions, so that users are only removed from this counter after they log off or their session times out. Determines current user load. MSExchange OWA\Current Unique Users
# There are many problems with that performance counter - it can starts to show huge numbers of connected users randomly. So trigger is disabled by default.
"\MSExchange OWA\Current Unique Users" "\9312\9316"

trigger.name: Perfomance Counter for OWA Unique Users
trigger.description:
Счётчик "\MSExchange OWA\Current Unique Users" показывает дичь. Известный баг, iisreset помогает.
Триггер для наблюдения за поведением счётчика!

item.name: OWA requests per second
item.description:
Shows the number of requests handled by Outlook Web App per second. Determines current user load. MSExchange OWA\Requests/sec
"\MSExchange OWA\Requests/sec" "\9312\9384"

item.name: Shows the number of messages in the active mailbox queues.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Active Mailbox Delivery Queue Length"]

trigger.name: Active Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Active Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Active Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Active Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Poison Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Poison Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in the drop directory used by a Foreign connector.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Active Non-Smtp Delivery Queue Length"]

trigger.name: Active Non-Smtp Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Active Non-Smtp Delivery Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Active Non-Smtp Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Active Non-Smtp Delivery Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in the active remote delivery queues.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Active Remote Delivery Queue Length"]

trigger.name: Active Remote Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Active Remote Delivery Queue Length is high. ttp://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Active Remote Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Active Remote Delivery Queue Length is too high. ttp://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages queued for delivery in all queues.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Aggregate Delivery Queue Length (All Queues)"]

trigger.name: Aggregate Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Aggregate Delivery Queue Length is higher then the microsoft recommednded. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Aggregate Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Aggregate Delivery Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in the largest delivery queues.

trigger.name: Largest Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Largest Delivery Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Largest Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Largest Delivery Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in a retry state attempting to deliver a message to a remote mailbox.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Retry Mailbox Delivery Queue Length"]

trigger.name: Retry Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Retry Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Retry Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Retry Mailbox Delivery Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in a retry state in the non-SMTP gateway delivery queues.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Retry Non-Smtp Delivery Queue Length"]

item.name: Shows the number of messages in a retry state in the remote delivery queues.
item.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchangeTransport Queues(_total)\Retry Remote Delivery Queue Length"]

trigger.name: Retry Remote Delivery Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Retry Remote Delivery Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Retry Remote Delivery Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Retry Remote Delivery Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in the submission queue.

trigger.name: Submission Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Submission Queue Length is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Submission Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Submission Queue Length is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: Shows the number of messages in the Unreachable queue.

trigger.name: Unreachable Queue Length is high.
trigger.description: Unreachable Queue is high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

trigger.name: Unreachable Queue Length is too high.
trigger.description: Unreachable Queue is too high. http://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff367923.aspx

item.name: MSExchangeWS requests per second
item.description:
Shows the number of requests processed each second. Determines current user load. MSExchangeWS\Requests/sec
"\MSExchangeWS\Requests/sec" "\3908\4402"

item.name: Web Service Default Web Site Current Connections
item.description:
Shows the current number of connections established to the Default website which corresponds to the number of connections hitting the Front End CAS server role. Determines current user load. Web Service(Default Web Site)\Current Connections
"\Web Service(Default Web Site)\Current Connections" "\2104(Default Web Site)\2146"

item.name: Processes Microsoft.Exchange.Store.Worker.exe count
Processes Microsoft.Exchange.Store.Worker.exe wkset summary
Processes MSExchangeFrontendTransport.exe wkset
Processes MSExchangeTransport.exe wkset

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} database copy status
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Active Manager({#EDB.NAME})\Database Copy Role Active"]
item_prototype.description:
Is the database {#DB.NAME} copy active? 0 - passive 1 - active
"\MSExchange Active Manager({#EDB.NAME})\Database Copy Role Active" "\11196({#EDB.NAME})\11200"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} database mounted state
item_prototype.description:
Database {#DB.NAME} mounted state. 0 - not mounted 1 - mounted
"\MSExchange Active Manager({#EDB.NAME})\Database Mounted" "\11196({#EDB.NAME})\11198"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} page faults per second
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Database(Information Store - {#EDB.NAME})\Database Page Fault Stalls/sec",{$PERIOD}]
item_prototype.description:
Indicates the rate of page faults that can't be serviced because there are no pages available for allocation from the database cache. If this counter is above 0, it's an indication that the MSExchange Database\I/O Database Writes (Attached) Average Latency is too high.
"\MSExchange Database(Information Store - {#DB.NAME})\Database Page Fault Stalls/sec" "\12654(Information Store - {#DB.NAME})\13042"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} log record stalls
item_prototype.description:
Indicates the number of log records that can't be added to the log buffers per second because the log buffers are full.
The average value should be below 10 per second. Spikes (maximum values) shouldn't be higher than 100 per second.
"\MSExchange Database(Information Store - {#EDB.NAME})\Log Record Stalls/sec" "\12654(Information Store - {#EDB.NAME})\12862"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} log threads waiting
item_prototype.description:
Indicates the number of threads waiting to complete an update of the database by writing their data to the log.
The average value should be less than 10 threads waiting.
"\MSExchange Database(Information Store - {#EDB.NAME})\Log Threads Waiting" "\12654(Information Store - {#EDB.NAME})\12816"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} active database read operations per second
item_prototype.description:
Shows the number of database read operations per second for each attached database instance. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Reads (Attached)/sec
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Reads (Attached)/sec" "\13572({#EDB.NAME}/_Total)\14024"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} active database read operations latency
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Reads (Attached) Average Latency",{$PERIOD}]
item_prototype.description:
Shows the average length of time, in milliseconds (ms), per database read operation. Should be less than 20ms on average. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Reads (Attached) Average Latency
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Reads (Attached) Average Latency" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14026"

trigger_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} read time latency too high: {ITEM.VALUE}
trigger_prototype.description: "{#DB.NAME}" average read time latency too high for 5 min.

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} passive database read operation latency
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Reads (Recovery) Average Latency",{$PERIOD}]
item_prototype.description:
Shows the average length of time, in ms, per passive database read operation. Should be less than 200ms on average. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Reads (Recovery) Average Latency
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Reads (Recovery) Average Latency" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14042"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} active database writes operations per second
item_prototype.description:
Shows the number of database write operations per second for each attached database instance. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Writes (Attached)/sec
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Writes (Attached)/sec" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14088"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME}active database writes operations latency
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Writes (Attached) Average Latency",{$PERIOD}]
item_prototype.description:
Shows the average length of time, in ms, per database write operation. Should be less than 50ms on average. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Writes (Attached) Average Latency
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Writes (Attached) Average Latency" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14090"

trigger_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} write time latency too high: {ITEM.VALUE}
trigger_prototype.description: "{#DB.NAME}" average write latency too high. In general, this latency should be less than the "MSExchange Database\I/O Database Reads (Attached) Average Latency" when battery-backed write caching is utilized.

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} passive database writes operation latency
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Writes (Recovery) Average Latency",{$PERIOD}]
item_prototype.description:
Shows the average length of time, in ms, per passive database write operation. Should be less than the read latency for the same instance, as measured by the MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Reads (Recovery) Average Latency counter. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Database Writes (Recovery) Average Latency
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Database Writes (Recovery) Average Latency" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14106"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} log writes operations per second
item_prototype.description:
Shows the number of log writes per second for each database instance. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Log Writes/sec
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Log Writes/sec" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14136"

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} log writes operations latency
item_prototype.key: perf_counter_en["\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Log Writes Average Latency",{$PERIOD}]
item_prototype.description:
Shows the average length of time, in ms, per Log write operation. Should be less than 10ms on average. MSExchange Database ==> Instances(\*)\I/O Log Writes Average Latency
"\MSExchange Database ==> Instances({#EDB.NAME})\I/O Log Writes Average Latency" "\13572({#EDB.NAME})\14138"

trigger_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} log write latency too high: {ITEM.VALUE}
trigger_prototype.description: Log writes latency too high for database "{#DB.NAME}".

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} active mailboxes count
item_prototype.description: Database {#EDB.NAME} number of active mailboxes.

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} database state
item_prototype.description: Database {#EDB.NAME} state

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} RPC requests latency
item_prototype.description: RPC Latency average (msec) is the average latency in milliseconds of RPC requests per database. Average is calculated over all RPCs since exrpc32 was loaded. Should be less than 50ms at all times, with spikes less than 100ms.

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} RPC requests per second
item_prototype.description: Shows the number of RPC operations per second for each database instance.

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} RPC requests total
item_prototype.description: Indicates the overall RPC requests currently executing within the information store process. Should be below 70 at all times.

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} content index state
item_prototype.description: Database {#EDB.NAME} content index state.

valuemap.name: Exchange Server 2016 Database Content Index States

item_prototype.name: {#EDB.NAME} edb file size
item_prototype.description: Database {#DB.NAME} edb file size.

value_map.name: Exchange Server 2016 Database Content Index States
